search_queries: 2026 Social Security retirement earnings test exempt amount SSA
2026 Social Security annual earnings limit before full retirement age
SSA 2026 COLA fact sheet earnings test thresholds
contract_notes: The earnings test has two age brackets: (1) under FRA for the entire year, (2) the year FRA is reached (counts earnings only in months before the FRA month).
Report both annual and monthly exempt amounts. The monthly amount equals the annual amount divided by 12 and is used for the special first-year rule.
The under-FRA reduction rate is $1 withheld per $2 over the limit (0.5). The year-of-FRA reduction rate is $1 withheld per $3 over the limit (1/3).
Reduction rates are statutory (Social Security Act §203) and do not change annually. Exempt amounts are adjusted annually with the national average wage index.
Starting with the month FRA is reached, there is no earnings test — benefits are no longer withheld regardless of earnings.
This entry does not include the post-FRA benefit recalculation (Adjustment of Reduction Factor) that restores previously withheld benefits.
